title: Hydrogen chloride
summary: One hydrogen and one chlorine. As a gas it is hydrogen chloride; dissolved in water it is hydrochloric acid, a strong acid that donates its $\mathrm{H^+}$ completely — the acid in the neutralization lesson. The name changing with phase is a convention, not a change in the molar mass, which sums the two sourced weights either way.
notes: 'Hydrogen chloride' (the molecule) and 'hydrochloric acid' (its water solution) are the same formula named two ways — a Phase-2 acid-naming topic.
